tool.name: dashing2 dist
tool.description: compare sketches or sequences into a distance/similarity matrix
tool.command:
#import re ## Build a file-of-files of symlinks named by element_identifier (see the ## sketch tool for the rationale). Inputs may be raw sequences or sketches ## produced by dashing2 sketch; dashing2 dist detects which and sketches ## raw inputs on the fly using the sketch options below. mkdir -p '@FFILE_DIR@' && #for $element in $reference_collection: #set $name = re.sub('[^A-Za-z0-9._-]', '_', str($element.element_identifier)) ln -s '$element' '@FFILE_DIR@/${name}' && echo '@FFILE_DIR@/${name}' >> 'ffile.txt' && #end for #set $use_query = $query.use_query == 'yes' #if $use_query: #for $qelement in $query.query_collection: #set $qname = re.sub('[^A-Za-z0-9._-]', '_', str($qelement.element_identifier)) ln -s '$qelement' '@FFILE_DIR@/${qname}' && echo '@FFILE_DIR@/${qname}' >> 'qfile.txt' && #end for #end if
dashing2 dist -p \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-8} -S${sketch_params.sketch_size} $alphabet $sketch_mode #if str($sketch_params.kmer_length) != '': -k${sketch_params.kmer_length} #end if #if str($sketch_params.window_size) != '': --window-size ${sketch_params.window_size} #end if #if str($sketch_params.spacing) != '': --spacing '${sketch_params.spacing}' #end if $sketch_params.no_canon $sketch_params.long_kmers #if str($sketch_params.seed) != '': --seed ${sketch_params.seed} #end if $parse_by_seq $measure #if not $use_query: $result_shape #end if #if $sparse.mode == 'topk': --topk ${sparse.topk} #elif $sparse.mode == 'threshold': --similarity-threshold ${sparse.threshold} #end if $binary_output #if $use_query: -Q 'qfile.txt' #end if --cmpout '$output' -F 'ffile.txt'

param.name: reference_collection
param.label: Reference collection
param.help: A collection of datasets to compare. These may be raw FASTA/FASTQ sequences (sketched on the fly) or sketches produced by the dashing2 sketch tool. Two elements give a single pairwise distance; N elements give an all-pairs matrix.

param.name: use_query
param.label: Rectangular query-vs-reference comparison
param.help: Compare every reference dataset against a separate query collection, producing a rectangular |reference|×|query| matrix instead of an all-pairs matrix.

param.name: query_collection
param.label: Query collection
param.help: Datasets forming the query set. Each is compared against every dataset in the reference collection.

param.label: Parse each sequence separately
param.help: Treat every sequence within every raw input file as a separate entity rather than one entity per file. Only relevant when the inputs are raw sequences.

tool.help:
dashing2 dist compares a set of inputs and emits a distance or similarity matrix. It is the comparison half of dashing2: it accepts either raw sequences (which it sketches on the fly) or sketches already produced by the *dashing2 sketch* tool.
This is equivalent to ``dashing2 cmp``. Use the *dashing2 sketch* tool instead when you want to keep the sketches for reuse; use this tool when you only need the matrix.
-----
Input *****
A **reference collection** of datasets. Each element may be a raw FASTA/FASTQ sequence or a dashing2 sketch (``binary``); the two can be mixed and dashing2 detects which is which. The element names become the row/column labels.
* **Two elements** yield a single pairwise distance. * **N elements** yield an all-pairs matrix. * Supplying a **query collection** yields a rectangular reference-vs-query matrix.
When the inputs are raw sequences, the **Sketch construction**, **Sketching mode** and **Sequence alphabet** options control how they are sketched (identical to the *dashing2 sketch* tool); they are ignored for inputs that are already sketches.
-----
Options *******
=========================== ========================== =========== =========================== **Option** **Flag** **Default** **Description** --------------------------- -------------------------- ----------- --------------------------- Comparison measure --distance / --containment similarity Quantity emitted per pair Output shape --asymmetric-all-pairs symmetric Dense matrix layout Sparse Top-k --topk off Nearest-neighbour graph Sparse threshold --similarity-threshold off Thresholded similarity graph Binary output --binary-output False float32 instead of text =========================== ========================== =========== ===========================
See the *dashing2 sketch* tool help for the full list of sketch-construction and sketching-mode parameters, which are shared between the two tools.
-----
Output ******
A single distance/similarity matrix. The symmetric all-pairs result is an upper-triangular PHYLIP matrix; square and rectangular results are flat tab-separated matrices; Top-k and threshold results are compressed-sparse-row (CSR) text. ``--binary-output`` replaces the text with a float32 binary matrix.
